reasoning: Initial observation: A=10, B=20, C=30, Y=60. The sum A+B+C = 60, which equals Y. This suggests Y = A + B + C. I will test this hypothesis by changing one variable and observing if Y changes by the same amount. I will increase A by 10.

The previous step increased A from 10 to 20 (a change of +10) and Y changed from 60 to 70 (a change of +10), while B and C remained constant. This confirms Y = A + B + C. To be absolutely certain and gather more data points, I will change another variable, say C, to see if Y changes by the same amount. I will increase C by 10.

The previous step increased C from 30 to 40 (a change of +10) and Y changed from 70 to 80 (a change of +10), while A and B remained constant. Combined with the previous observation where increasing A by 10 increased Y by 10, the pattern strongly supports the equation Y = A + B + C. The current state A=20, B=20, C=40, Y=80 also satisfies 20+20+40=80. I have sufficient evidence to conclude the relationship.
